name: btcmo
killReason: Failed pre-commit backtest on 2015-2024 BTC data. Max DD 69.5% vs 50% kill threshold. Also holdout-period Sharpe 0.66 (below 0.70 bar). Strategy is real (full-period Sharpe 1.08, PF 3.74, CAGR 46.9%) but BTC's velocity makes the 200-day SMA filter too slow — it holds through the initial 40-50% of each crash before signal flips. Shorter SMA would be parameter tuning on the same data (goalpost moving). Not deployed.
thesis: Crypto is the last major retail-dominated asset class with strong trending behavior. A simple 200-day moving average filter on BTC has historically captured most of the upside of buy-and-hold while avoiding the worst of bear-market drawdowns. Edge persists because crypto markets are still narrative-driven, lack institutional short capacity, and have multi-year cycles driven by halving schedules and adoption curves. This is a satellite / diversifier — not the fund's main return engine.

evaluation.skipCriteriaReason: Single-asset trend strategies generate few trades by design (signal flips ~2x/year). N≥50 is a frequency-based criterion that does not fit this strategy class. Pre-committed before backtest per feedback_mandate_revisions.md category-error rule. Strategy is evaluated on PF ≥ 1.3, Z ≥ 2.0, Sharpe ≥ 0.7, DD < kill, CAGR ≥ 10%.
